vocabulary.example: For context, the city's population doubled in just 20 years.
The project cost ten million euros, give or take.

vocabulary.translation: por así decirlo / en cierto sentido
vocabulary.example: The city is the engine room, so to speak, of the national economy.
The phenomenon, also known as urban sprawl, affects most big cities.

vocabulary.translation: que se remonta a / que tiene su origen en
vocabulary.example: The city has a historical centre that dates back to the 13th century.
In the long run, trust in political institutions is likely to improve.
On the whole, the project has been a great success.

vocabulary.translation: considerando todo / teniendo todo en cuenta
vocabulary.example: All things considered, the proposal deserves our support.
The traffic is terrible, not to mention the air pollution.

vocabulary.translation: completamente incapaz de ver / muy despistado
vocabulary.example: Without his glasses, he was as blind as a bat.

vocabulary.translation: muy atrevido o audaz / con mucha cara
vocabulary.example: Speaking up in the meeting, she was as bold as brass.
She was as busy as a bee, juggling work, family, and social commitments.
The lake was as calm as a millpond on a windless day.
The street market offered clothes as cheap as chips.
The kitchen was as clean as a whistle after hours of scrubbing.
The instructions were as clear as crystal.
Despite the crisis, he remained as cool as a cucumber.
After the accident, the machine was as dead as a doornail.
The desert was as dry as a bone, with no sign of rain for weeks.
The lecture was as dull as dishwater, putting everyone to sleep.
Solving the puzzle turned out to be as easy as pie.
The car sped away as fast as lightning.
After months of training, he was as fit as a fiddle.
The landscape was as flat as a pancake, stretching out for miles.
After retiring, he felt as free as a bird to travel the world.
After a good night's sleep, she woke up feeling as fresh as a daisy.
Despite his imposing appearance, he was as gentle as a lamb with children.
The students were as good as gold during the school assembly.
Fresh out of college, he was as green as grass in the corporate world.
After receiving the promotion, she was as happy as a clam.
The old carpenter's hands were as hard as nails.

vocabulary.translation: muy alterado / bajo el efecto de drogas o alcohol
vocabulary.example: After the party, he was as high as a kite and couldn't walk straight.
After a day of hiking, they were as hungry as wolves.

vocabulary.translation: muy ligero / que casi no pesa
vocabulary.example: The fabric was as light as a feather, perfect for a summer dress.
The applause was as loud as thunder when the performer finished.
He was as mad as a hornet when he discovered the broken window.
Her desk was always as neat as a pin, with everything in its place.
Before the big presentation, she was as nervous as a cat.

vocabulary.translation: muy antiguo / que lleva mucho tiempo
vocabulary.example: The village was as old as the hills, with buildings that had stood for centuries.

vocabulary.translation: muy pálido / blanco como el papel
vocabulary.example: She turned as pale as a ghost when she saw the accident.
The truth was as plain as day, evident to anyone who looked.
The library was as quiet as a mouse, with everyone focused on their studies.
After a good night's sleep, she felt as right as rain.
Despite his age, he remained as sharp as a tack.
The traffic was as slow as molasses during rush hour.
The con artist was as sly as a fox, fooling everyone with his schemes.
The lotion left her skin as smooth as silk.
The foundation of the building was as solid as a rock.
Despite his age, he remained as strong as an ox.
Despite the convincing arguments, he remained as stubborn as a mule.
Her voice was as sweet as honey, soothing everyone in the room.
Despite eating a lot, he remained as thin as a rail.
The marathon runner was as tough as nails.

vocabulary.word: as useful as a chocolate teapot
vocabulary.example: His advice was as useful as a chocolate teapot — no help at all.
The fireplace made the room as warm as toast on a cold winter night.

vocabulary.translation: muy pálido / blanco por el miedo o el shock
vocabulary.example: When she heard the news, her face turned as white as a sheet.
The professor was as wise as an owl.

vocabulary.word: like a bat out of hell
vocabulary.translation: a una velocidad enorme / como alma que lleva el diablo
vocabulary.example: When the alarm went off, he ran out of the building like a bat out of hell.

vocabulary.word: like a bull in a china shop
vocabulary.translation: muy torpe / que actúa con descuido en situaciones delicadas
vocabulary.example: He entered the elegant party like a bull in a china shop.

vocabulary.word: like a cat on a hot tin roof
vocabulary.example: Waiting for the exam results, she was pacing like a cat on a hot tin roof.

vocabulary.word: like a dog with a bone
vocabulary.example: She pursued the truth like a dog with a bone.

vocabulary.word: like a fish out of water
vocabulary.example: In the city, he felt like a fish out of water.

vocabulary.word: like a moth to a flame
vocabulary.translation: irresistiblemente atraído hacia algo aunque sea peligroso
vocabulary.example: She was like a moth to a flame when it came to risky adventures.

vocabulary.word: like a needle in a haystack
vocabulary.example: Searching for the lost key in the park was like looking for a needle in a haystack.
The news hit him like a ton of bricks, leaving him stunned.

vocabulary.word: like a wolf in sheep's clothing
vocabulary.translation: engañoso / que oculta sus verdaderas intenciones
vocabulary.example: The charming stranger turned out to be like a wolf in sheep's clothing.
Her emotions were like an open book, evident in her facial expressions.

vocabulary.translation: muy difícil / que requiere un gran esfuerzo
vocabulary.example: Getting information from the reluctant witness was like pulling teeth.

vocabulary.word: like two peas in a pod
vocabulary.example: The twins were like two peas in a pod in looks and personality.

vocabulary.word: like water off a duck's back
vocabulary.translation: sin efecto alguno / que no afecta
vocabulary.example: Criticism rolled off her like water off a duck's back.
After the long hike, I slept like a log.

vocabulary.translation: muy suave / fácil y sin complicaciones
vocabulary.example: The negotiations went smooth as butter, reaching a quick agreement.

vocabulary.translation: objetivos y metas / propósito de un informe
vocabulary.example: The introduction should clearly state the aims and objectives of the report.

vocabulary.translation: metodología / técnicas usadas para recopilar datos
vocabulary.example: The methodology section should describe how the data was collected.
The findings suggest that more investment in public transport is needed.
The conclusion should include specific recommendations for improvement.
A good report must be concise and well-structured.
A proposal must be persuasive to gain the reader's approval.
The report should be factual and avoid personal opinions.

vocabulary.translation: estructura formal / organización rígida y profesional
vocabulary.example: Reports and proposals require a formal structure with clear sections.
It is recommended that the council reviews its housing policy.

vocabulary.translation: a la luz de los resultados / teniendo en cuenta los hallazgos
vocabulary.example: In light of the findings, we propose a series of improvements.
To sum up, the proposal offers a viable solution to the problem.
As outlined above, the three key issues are funding, planning and timing.

vocabulary.translation: por lo tanto, se propone que
vocabulary.example: It is therefore proposed that the budget be increased by 15%.

vocabulary.translation: con esto en mente / teniendo esto en cuenta
vocabulary.example: With this in mind, we recommend a phased approach to development.
This report aims to assess the current state of public transport.

vocabulary.translation: tras reflexionar / después de considerar el asunto
vocabulary.example: Upon reflection, the committee decided to revise its initial proposal.

vocabulary.translation: ensayo discursivo / redacción que explora distintos puntos de vista
vocabulary.example: Unlike a report, a discursive essay allows more personal expression.

vocabulary.translation: conectores de contraste (however, nevertheless, on the other hand...)
vocabulary.example: Use linkers of contrast to balance arguments in formal writing.

vocabulary.translation: conectores de consecuencia (therefore, as a result, consequently...)
vocabulary.example: Linkers of consequence help show cause-and-effect relationships.
I hope to be recognized for my work.
She expects to be invited to the party.
I want to be promoted soon.

vocabulary.translation: need → infinitivo pasivo (foco en la acción)
vocabulary.example: This machine needs to be repaired urgently.
The problem deserves to be discussed in more detail.
All applicants are required to be informed in advance.
We arranged to be met at the airport.
The system doesn't seem to be updated with the latest software.
She agreed to be interviewed by the committee.
He chose to be represented by a lawyer.
She prefers to be called by her first name.
I hate to be the last one to know.
I can't afford to go on holiday.
She managed to finish the report on time.
He refused to sign the contract.
They failed to submit the documents on time.
She promised to call as soon as she arrived.
He pretended not to notice the mistake.
I really enjoyed being involved in the project.
She avoids being seen in public.

vocabulary.translation: remember → gerundio pasivo (recuerdo de algo recibido)
vocabulary.example: I remember being told I wasn't clever enough.
I don't recall being informed about this issue.

vocabulary.translation: hate → gerundio pasivo (proceso en sí, sin gustar)
vocabulary.example: My nephew hates being teased by his classmates.
She doesn't mind being asked difficult questions.
They considered being transferred to another branch.
I dislike being interrupted when I'm speaking.
They risk being fined if they don't comply.
She kept being asked the same question.
He always insisted on being called 'Sir'.
She succeeded in being selected for the position.
He admitted making a mistake in the report.
She denied knowing anything about the incident.
Have you finished writing the proposal?
They suggested reducing traffic in the city centre.
The report recommends introducing stricter regulations.
This proposal involves improving cycling infrastructure.
They considered reducing traffic in the city centre.
She kept putting off making the decision.
He gave up trying to fix the machine.
Do you fancy going out for dinner tonight?
She can't stand being kept waiting.
I can't help feeling that something is wrong.
You should practise speaking in front of a mirror.
I can't imagine living without access to the internet.

vocabulary.translation: by + gerundio / medio para lograr algo
vocabulary.example: This can be achieved by investing in public transport.
They left without informing anyone about the decision.

vocabulary.translation: instead of + gerundio / en lugar de hacer algo
vocabulary.example: Instead of using cars, people could cycle to work.

vocabulary.translation: after + gerundio / después de hacer algo
vocabulary.example: After reviewing the data, we reached a conclusion.

vocabulary.translation: before + gerundio / antes de hacer algo
vocabulary.example: Before submitting the proposal, check all the headings.

vocabulary.translation: for + gerundio / razón o propósito (❌ nunca for + to-inf)
vocabulary.example: The city is known for investing heavily in green technology.

vocabulary.translation: in spite of + gerundio / a pesar de hacer algo
vocabulary.example: In spite of having read the instructions twice, I couldn't understand it.
On arriving at the hotel, he went straight to his room.

vocabulary.translation: about + gerundio / a punto de / referido a
vocabulary.example: She was worried about being left out of the project.

vocabulary.translation: of + gerundio / después de sustantivo o adjetivo
vocabulary.example: There is a risk of being misunderstood if the tone is too formal.

vocabulary.translation: remember + -ing → recuerdo de algo ya hecho
vocabulary.example: I remember being told I wasn't clever enough. (= recuerdo que me lo dijeron)

vocabulary.translation: remember + to-inf → acordarse de hacer algo (obligación)
vocabulary.example: Did you remember to ring Nigel? (= ¿te acordaste de llamar?)
She stopped crying as soon as she saw her mother.

vocabulary.translation: stop + to-inf → pararse para hacer otra cosa
vocabulary.example: We stopped to buy some water at the motorway service area.

vocabulary.translation: try + -ing → probar a hacer algo (a ver qué pasa)
vocabulary.example: I tried searching the web and finally found an address.

vocabulary.translation: try + to-inf → intentar hacer algo (con dificultad)
vocabulary.example: I tried to email Simon but it bounced back.

vocabulary.translation: go on + -ing → continuar haciendo lo mismo
vocabulary.example: He went on singing after everyone else had finished.

vocabulary.translation: go on + to-inf → pasar a hacer algo diferente
vocabulary.example: She recited a poem, then went on to sing a lovely folk song.
Working in London means leaving home at 6.30.

vocabulary.translation: mean + to-inf → tener la intención de
vocabulary.example: I didn't mean to make you cry.

vocabulary.translation: regret + -ing → lamentarse de algo ya hecho
vocabulary.example: I regret saying that — it was unkind.

vocabulary.translation: regret + to-inf → lamentar tener que decir algo (formal)
vocabulary.example: We regret to inform you that your application was unsuccessful.
Running to the bus stop, he tripped over.

vocabulary.translation: participio pasado / cláusula pasiva reducida → past participle
vocabulary.example: Directed by Spielberg, the movie was a huge success.

vocabulary.translation: participio perfecto / acción anterior → having + past participle
vocabulary.example: Having studied at university, I got a job in research.

vocabulary.translation: participio perfecto pasivo → having been + past participle
vocabulary.example: Having been educated at Eton, he had a good start in life.

vocabulary.translation: participio presente pasivo → being + past participle
vocabulary.example: Being trained as a doctor, she was often asked to help in emergencies.
❌ They suggested to reduce traffic. ✅ They suggested reducing traffic.

vocabulary.translation: Tras preposición SIEMPRE -ing (by, without, instead of, for...)
vocabulary.example: ❌ This can be achieved by invest more money. ✅ by investing more money.

vocabulary.word: ❌ needs to address → ✅ needs to be addressed
vocabulary.translation: need/deserve/require + to be + past participle (pasivo, no activo)
vocabulary.example: ❌ This issue needs to address. ✅ This issue needs to be addressed.

vocabulary.translation: for + -ing SIEMPRE (❌ for + to-infinitive es calco del español)
vocabulary.example: ❌ for improve safety. ✅ for improving safety.

vocabulary.word: ❌ the aim is improve → ✅ the aim is to improve
vocabulary.example: ❌ The aim is improve traffic. ✅ The aim is to improve traffic.

vocabulary.word: ❌ people is used to drive → ✅ people are used to driving
vocabulary.translation: be used to + -ing (estar acostumbrado), NOT to-infinitive
vocabulary.example: ❌ people is used to drive. ✅ people are used to driving.

vocabulary.word: ❌ this needs being solved → ✅ this needs to be solved
vocabulary.translation: need + to be + past participle (pasivo formal) / need + -ing (compacto, informal)
vocabulary.example: ❌ This needs being solved. ✅ This needs to be solved. / This needs solving.
❌ They left without to inform anyone. ✅ without informing anyone.

vocabulary.word: ❌ people should be encouraged to be helped → ✅ encouraged to help
vocabulary.translation: No doble pasivo innecesario: encouraged to + active infinitive
vocabulary.example: ❌ People should be encouraged to be helped. ✅ People should be encouraged to help.

vocabulary.word: ❌ gain a scholarship → ✅ apply for a scholarship
vocabulary.translation: Colocación incorrecta: gain/get → apply for a scholarship
vocabulary.example: ❌ She managed to gain a scholarship. ✅ She applied for a scholarship.

vocabulary.word: ❌ make the homologation → ✅ complete the homologation process
vocabulary.translation: make no colocaciona con procesos formales: complete/start/go through
vocabulary.example: ❌ She needs to make the homologation. ✅ complete the homologation process.

vocabulary.word: ❌ pass the documentation → ✅ submit the documentation
vocabulary.translation: submit = entregar documentos formalmente (pass = calco del español)
vocabulary.example: ❌ You need to pass the documentation. ✅ You need to submit the documentation.

vocabulary.translation: False friend: educative ≠ educativo. Correcto: education system
vocabulary.example: ❌ the educative system. ✅ the education system.

vocabulary.translation: False friend: antecedentes penales = criminal record (no penal antecedents)
vocabulary.example: ❌ He has penal antecedents. ✅ He has a criminal record.

vocabulary.translation: False friend: actual ≠ actual. actual (inglés) = real/verdadero. actual (español) = current
vocabulary.example: ❌ her actual level of English. ✅ her current level of English.

vocabulary.translation: False friend: formación = training or education (no formation)
vocabulary.example: ❌ She has a good formation. ✅ She has received excellent training.

vocabulary.translation: False friend: estudios superiores = higher education (no superior studies)
vocabulary.example: ❌ She completed her superior studies. ✅ She completed her higher education.

vocabulary.translation: False friend: compromiso = commitment. compromise (inglés) = acuerdo de concesiones mutuas
vocabulary.example: ❌ Due to my compromise with the project... ✅ Due to my commitment to the project...

vocabulary.translation: False friend: empático = empathetic. emphatic (inglés) = enfático, contundente
vocabulary.example: ❌ She is very emphatic with her students. ✅ She is very empathetic.
My phone has a long-lasting battery.
Tom is an easy-going student who never stresses about exams.
She introduced us to her good-looking boyfriend.

vocabulary.translation: que se mueve lentamente / de avance lento
vocabulary.example: We were stuck on a slow-moving train for hours.

vocabulary.translation: holgado / que no ajusta al cuerpo
vocabulary.example: I prefer wearing loose-fitting clothing in summer.
Renewable energy is a fast-growing industry worldwide.
She manages a high-performing team of engineers.

vocabulary.translation: de gran alcance / con consecuencias amplias
vocabulary.example: The new policy will have far-reaching consequences.
She is one of the most hard-working students in the class.

vocabulary.translation: de larga trayectoria / que existe desde hace mucho
vocabulary.example: There is a long-standing tradition of debate in this school.
I've seen that short-haired woman somewhere before.
My father has very old-fashioned ideas about education.
They had a short-lived relationship that ended after two months.

vocabulary.translation: prefabricado / ya hecho / listo para usar
vocabulary.example: I eat a lot of ready-made meals during busy weeks.
She ordered some deep-fried chicken and chips.
He is a well-known actor in European cinema.

vocabulary.translation: miope / con poca visión de futuro
vocabulary.example: It would be short-sighted to cut funding for education.

vocabulary.translation: con los ojos muy abiertos / ingenuo / asombrado
vocabulary.example: She looked at the city wide-eyed, amazed by everything.
A fair-minded judge considers all the evidence carefully.

vocabulary.translation: de mente abierta / tolerante y receptivo
vocabulary.example: You need to be open-minded when living in a new culture.
She is extremely strong-willed and never gives up.
Narrow-minded attitudes make it hard to accept change.
John is a long-distance runner who trains every day.

vocabulary.translation: de última hora / en el último momento
vocabulary.example: It was a last-minute decision that changed everything.
He signed a short-term contract with the company.

vocabulary.translation: de cuerpo entero / de longitud completa
vocabulary.example: She wore a full-length skirt to the formal dinner.
The proposal focuses on delivering high-quality public services.
She works full-time and also studies in the evenings.
He has a part-time job at the local library.
The city undertook a large-scale regeneration project.

vocabulary.translation: a pequeña escala / de ámbito reducido
vocabulary.example: They started with a small-scale pilot scheme first.
Investing in new technology can be a high-risk strategy.
Filling in all these forms is a very time-consuming task.
Australia is an English-speaking country.
It was a record-breaking achievement in the history of science.

vocabulary.translation: apetitoso / que hace la boca agua
vocabulary.example: She made us a mouth-watering meal with fresh ingredients.

vocabulary.translation: que hace reflexionar / provocador de ideas
vocabulary.example: It was a thought-provoking lecture on climate change.
Studying abroad was a life-changing experience for her.
The scientists published a ground-breaking study last year.
The council introduced several money-saving measures.
The poster was eye-catching and generated a lot of interest.

vocabulary.translation: que llena el corazón / emotivo y positivo
vocabulary.example: It was a heart-warming story about community spirit.
She brought us some home-made bread from her village.
These sun-dried tomatoes add a rich flavour to the dish.
My father is a middle-aged man with a lot of energy.

vocabulary.translation: de energía eólica / impulsado por el viento
vocabulary.example: The island is entirely wind-powered.
We drove through snow-covered mountains on our way north.
She gave me a hand-made card for my birthday.
The students live in a purpose-built residence near campus.
The report accused them of state-sponsored espionage.
I feel like an ice-cold beer on a hot day like this.
He is a world-famous author translated into 40 languages.
Dan is on a sugar-free diet for health reasons.
Spinach is an iron-rich vegetable recommended for anaemia.

vocabulary.translation: libre de crueldad / no testado en animales
vocabulary.example: I only buy cruelty-free cosmetics.

vocabulary.translation: libre de impuestos / exento de impuestos
vocabulary.example: Duty-free shops sell tax-free goods at airports.

vocabulary.translation: libre de humo / donde no se puede fumar
vocabulary.example: The city introduced smoke-free zones in public spaces.
He lived in the flat rent-free for a year while renovating it.
She is a very well-educated woman with three degrees.
He is a highly-respected figure in the scientific community.

vocabulary.translation: bien educado / que se porta bien
vocabulary.example: They have two well-behaved children who are a pleasure to teach.
We sat in a brightly-lit room during the interview.
India is a densely-populated country with over a billion people.
The examiner praised her for a well-written proposal.
The app failed because it had a poorly-designed interface.
It is a well-established fact that exercise improves mental health.
This is a widely-accepted theory in modern linguistics.
She finally found a well-paid job in the tech sector.
Many care workers are badly-paid despite their vital role.
It is a well-known fact that sleep deprivation affects performance.
Let's take a five-minute break before we continue.
He signed a one-year contract with the research institute.
I live in a six-storey building in the city centre.
She rents a two-room flat near the university.
Most people in Europe work a five-day week.
She has a ten-year-old daughter who loves science.
They bought a three-bedroom house on the outskirts.
She won the 100-metre race at the regional championships.
Spiders are eight-legged creatures, not insects.
A cyclops is a one-eyed creature from Greek mythology.
He met a one-armed man who had lost his arm in an accident.
The two-headed eagle is a symbol used in many national flags.
A rhinoceros is sometimes called a three-horned animal.

vocabulary.translation: Usar guión cuando el compound adjective va ANTES del sustantivo
vocabulary.example: He is a well-known actor. ✅ / He is well known. (sin guión tras verbo be)

vocabulary.translation: Sin guión cuando va DESPUÉS de be, look, seem, taste...
vocabulary.example: The actor is well known. ✅ / The friendship was short lived. ✅

vocabulary.translation: Adverbios en -ly NO llevan guión (excepto well-)
vocabulary.example: A beautifully decorated room ✅ (not beautifully-decorated) / A well-trained athlete ✅

vocabulary.translation: En number+noun: número en letras y sustantivo en SINGULAR
vocabulary.example: A five-minute break ✅ / A 5-minutes break ❌ / A three-bedroom house ✅

vocabulary.translation: de última generación / más avanzado disponible (3+ palabras con guiones)
vocabulary.example: They used state-of-the-art technology in their research.

vocabulary.translation: de dos años (estructura número-año-old con guiones)
vocabulary.example: She has a two-year-old daughter who is learning to read.
